attributes.description: Alerts when the buffer cache hit ratio drops below 95%. A low ratio means disk reads are replacing cache hits — memory pressure.
attributes.name: [SQL Server OTel] Buffer cache hit ratio low

investigation_guide.blob:
## SQL Server buffer cache hit ratio low

### What fired
Buffer cache hit ratio dropped below 95% during the window.

### Why it matters
SQL Server serves most pages from the buffer pool. A dropping hit ratio means more reads are forced to disk — a classic memory-pressure signal.

### Triage
1. Check page life expectancy and free-list stalls.
2. Inspect memory grants pending.
3. Look for large queries pushing buffer pool.

### Remediation
- Add memory.
- Tune problem queries.

### Tuning
- 95% threshold; some workloads tolerate 90-95%.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-sqlserverreceiver.otel-default
// Instance-level metric: buffer cache hit ratio as percentage (0-100)
| WHERE metrics.sqlserver.page.buffer_cache.hit_ratio IS NOT NULL
| STATS min_ratio = MIN(metrics.sqlserver.page.buffer_cache.hit_ratio),
       instance_id = MAX(resource.attributes.service.instance.id)
  BY COALESCE(resource.attributes.service.instance.id, host.name, "unknown")
// Ideally > 95%; drops mean disk reads replacing cache hits
| WHERE min_ratio < 95.0
| KEEP instance_id, min_ratio
